We are not able to take many important decisions because we are not married
To take the right decision we need to become fathers because that brings maturity
Our parents tell us to concentrate on our studies
Since our fathers take care of us so what is the need for us to take any decision
We can take decisions related o our studies but only in consultation with our parents
We do all the different types of agricultural activities but still we don’t take any decision since our parents are more experienced than us
Sometimes we participate in the decision making process
Education is a must and we all go to school
We go to school for knowledge so that we can build our future
If we are educated then we can give better future to our children
Education is important to conduct ourselves better in our day to day activities and in managing our families
Educated people are well mannered
Educated people sign where the illiterates out their thumb impression
I want to study a lot and make my family and my community proud
80% of the boys study till grade 12 and 95% of the girls study till grade 10.
5% of the girls study till grade 12 and 5% of the boys complete their bachelors
College is about 18 kms from our village and so girls are unable to commute that far
Girls don’t have cycle; they don’t know how to ride a cycle
Many boys and girls don’t go to school because of poverty
Parents always fear that boys may misbehave with their girls
Boys should complete their bachelors and girls should finish grade 12
Sometimes girls get married after grade 10/12
Sometimes poor people decide not to send their daughters to school
No parents think differently when it comes to educating their daughters and sons; they are more concerned that their sons study hard and complete their education
Parents also worry about eve teasing and such safety issues especially since the college is so far off
Poor people prefer to send their sons to school since they cannot afford to send both their sons and daughters
They also think that sons will get good jobs post studies and support them
Boys do all agricultural activities from planting to harvesting
Girls don’t go the field; they stay at home and help with household chores

Girls: Lack of proper transport and early marriage

I want to be a doctor
Most of us want good government or private job and be able to support our families

They are working as labor

“kheti hee yahan to gaon mein” (there is only agriculture here)

For Women: “Combine” as women usually are only associated with harvesting of wheat

Important wheat innovation for [sex of FGD]  NowZero tillerRotavatorJaivik FertilizerHybrid seeds
Important wheat innovation  for [sex of FGD] 10 years agoZero tillerHybrid seeds
Difficult to say what brought about these changes
Today lot of people come to the village from outside and hold meetings where they tell people about new developments in agriculture
Sometimes they have practical field demonstration
XX is spreading a lot of awareness
Once one person tries and experiences the benefits then others also jump on the bandwagon
Yes, roles are clearly defined
But mostly men do all agricultural activities; women are only involved with harvesting and sometimes spraying fertilizers
But young girls don’t do any work in the field
Young boys also only help out their elders but they mostly know all the activities whereas girls don’t
Sometimes girls help in digging (filed preparation) in corn cultivation
Girls are very delicate; they will not be able to work in scorching sun
How can they sell; their parents don’t send them to school after class 10; how would they allow them to grow and sell anything!!
Girls have no knowledge of agriculture
We (young men) know everything about agriculture as we help our parents in the fields
But it interferes with our study
But we are not as experienced as our fathers and therefore we cannot do it all by ourselves; we need our parents to guide us
But we will be allowed to do and we can do
In fact selling will be no problem for us
But money will be a problem
Usually families have one single plot; our fathers take all decisions
Sometimes women maintain small kitchen gardens in their backyard for which not much money is required and they usually grow vegetables for home consumption
But decisions are almost always taken by our fathers
Selling is always men’s domain
Men and women don’t have separate plots; there is one single plot and wives mostly help their husbands
Sometimes men get plots registered in their wives’ names so that there are no fights over property distribution
Women are generally found working in the fields during the harvesting season
and also during sowing season if using “chita vidhi” i.e. sowing by broadcast method
Most household members willingly help out whenever required but on men’s plots; women don’t have separate plots
Those women with kitchen gardens take care of it themselves; men may sometimes help out a little when their wives are busy
A woman must most certainly consult her husband before doing anything and she must also ask her in laws or other elders
Men don’t need to ask their wives but they consult their elders
Adam will not mind much because she will be doing it with a group of women
If women go in groups then Adam may allow Ana to go
What is need for Ana to go if Adam is around
Ana will find it very difficult if she goes to the market; it will be better if Adam goes
Society is not very kind to women stepping in men’s domain and Adam will only want to protect Ana
Ana will not be able to fetch the right price herself
Men and elders say “bahar jayegi to izzat gir jayegi” i.e. Ana will demean herself and earn bad reputation if she ventures out to the market
There may be bitter fights over this issue if Ana insists that she be allowed to go
Yes, Adam will understand because Ana also has to take care of all the household work
Given that women only help with harvesting so Adam does not require much help from his wife
But she will have to help if it is the harvesting season
People will speak badly of her character for donning the man’s mantle
People will make fun of her and her husband
You must be kidding (laughing)!! He is not man enough to stay at home and cook and clean
It all depends upon the social customs of the village
What social customs; such a thing is bizarre; they may both work but how can the husband do household chores
Elders of the village will not be kind towards this man; he will be ridiculed by all
“pati babuji babuji karke bulayega usko” (Husband will call her “Sir”)
Community will also praise her
People will say that she if taking care of her family and fulfilling her responsibilities well
Gender equality means equal treatment of men and women in every field including education and work opportunities
It means we respect our women
“Jitna beta ko man samman dete hain utna hee beti ko bhi dena chahiye” ( both sons and daughters should be treated equally)
Women are not given equal property rights but they are given dowry during marriage
When married women come visiting their parents they are pampered a lot
Women cook at home and know how much is needed to feed their family and David will let her take that decision
But they will surely discuss it and things may become heated if David does not agree with her
But David will not have problem so long as Diana has some extra produce to sell in market
Fights are common only in lean financial periods

Women don’t go to the market

“maan ne wala baat hoga to maanege nahin to kyon maanege” (he will agree if it is something reasonable; else why would he)
David will not trust her information and will check it our himself
If it is a profitable venture then David will agree
